subject: Mathematics
title: Paul Erdos: The Wandering Mathematician
description: A fast breakdown of Paul Erdos: The Wandering Mathematician for builders.

hook.text: Paul Erdos owned no house, carried one suitcase, and co-authored more papers than anyone in history.

buildup.text: He spent his life traveling from colleague to colleague, solving problems on their couches.

discovery.text: He published over 1,500 papers with more than 500 co-authors across dozens of countries.

twist.text: He lived on coffee and amphetamines, once quitting stimulants to win a bet—then calling the month unproductive.

climax.text: The 'Erdos number' measures collaboration distance from him. Most active mathematicians have a low one.

punchline.text: He had no home but every mathematician's door was open.
